codeReview.summary: The feature stays in scope and adds the requested integration suite, but the positive-path assertions stop at the internal follow-up queue instead of verifying that continuation is injected as a user-visible message in session history. That misses the feature contract's end-to-end observability requirement and can pass even when auto-dispatch to the transcript is broken.

issues.description: The suite introduces `getQueuedContinuationMessages()` and uses it in the positive-path tests (for example lines 157-159, 192, 255-259, 269, and 280-287 in commit `d6b6ee64`) to assert only `session.getFollowUpMessages()` / `pendingMessageCount`. The feature description and VAL-CONTINUATION-001 / 006 / 028 require end-to-end verification that the continuation appears as an injected user message via the harness observability helper (`harness.getInjectedUserMessages()` backed by `session.messages`). As written, these tests still pass when continuation never becomes a visible user message, so they do not fully cover the required behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker deviated from the prescribed baseline-validation command by running the repo-root `npm run test` before correcting to the manifest-defined coding-agent Vitest command. The skill may benefit from inlining the current `test-coding-agent` command from `.factory/services.yaml` in its startup checklist to reduce this recurring misread.

summary: Fail: the added tests validate queued follow-up state rather than the required injected user-message surface, so the feature does not fully satisfy its end-to-end continuation coverage contract.
